pub fn dump_proto(&self, _proto: &GcRef<LuaProto>, _writer: &mut dyn FnMut(&[u8]) -> Result<(), LuaError>, _strip: bool) -> Result<(), LuaError> { todo!("phase-b: dump_proto") }
-    pub fn trace_call(&mut self, _idx: CallInfoIdx) -> Result<bool, LuaError> { todo!("phase-b: trace_call") }
-    pub fn trace_exec(&mut self, _idx: CallInfoIdx, _pc: u32) -> Result<bool, LuaError> { todo!("phase-b: trace_exec") }
+    /// C: `int luaG_tracecall(lua_State *L)` — wrapper that returns the trap
+    /// flag as `bool` (C returns `int` 0/1).
+    /// The C function reads `L->ci` directly, so the `_idx` argument is unused;
+    /// the VM passes its locally tracked `ci` for symmetry with `trace_exec`.
+    pub fn trace_call(&mut self, _idx: CallInfoIdx) -> Result<bool, LuaError> {
+        Ok(crate::debug::trace_call(self)? != 0)
+    /// C: `int luaG_traceexec(lua_State *L, const Instruction *pc)` — wrapper
+    /// returning `bool` for the trap flag. `_idx` is unused for the same reason
+    /// as `trace_call`; `pc` is the 0-based index of the next instruction.
+    pub fn trace_exec(&mut self, _idx: CallInfoIdx, pc: u32) -> Result<bool, LuaError> {
+        Ok(crate::debug::trace_exec(self, pc)? != 0)

crate::do_::hookcall(self, idx)

delta.text: Build is

green.

**Report**

- **trace_call location**: `crates/lua-vm/src/state

.rs:2083` (`pub fn trace_call`)
- **What it does**: Thin wrapper that
delegates to the existing `crate::debug::trace_call`, converting the C-style `i32` (
0/1) trap flag into `bool`. Called from `vm.rs:1404` at the start of each Lua frame execution to fire the call hook when debug

ging is active.

**Family members also implemented in the same impl block:**
- `trace_exec` (state.rs:2084) — wraps `crate
::debug::trace_exec(self, pc)`, converts `i32` → `bool`. The natural sibling: same C source

area (`ldebug.c`), called paired with `trace_call` in the VM dispatch loop (`vm.rs:1415`).
- `get_
proto_instr` (state.rs:2080) — implemented as `ci.l
ua_closure().proto.code[pc]`. Not strictly the trace family, but unblocked because
`crate::debug::trace_exec` calls it at `debug.rs:1816`, so trace_exec would have
panicked on the first invocation. Changed generic `<T, P>` signature to concrete `(CallInfoI

dx, u32)` since the lone call site (`debug.rs:1816`) already passes those types.

**Newly-surfaced todos no

ticed but not implemented (different families):**
- `dump_proto` (state.rs:2081) — proto serializ

er family, separate ticket.
- `ci_prev_instruction`, `ci_prev2_instruction`, `ci_skip_next_instruction`, `
ci_step_pc_back`, `get_ci_pcrel`, `get_ci_vararg_info`, `get_ci_lua_proto_num

params` (state.rs:1538–1553) — `ci_*` accessor family.
- `CallInfo::is_vararg_func
`, `set_recover_status`, `recover_status` (state.rs:312–328) — CallInfo struct family

.
- `get_prev_ci`, `ci_next_func` (state.rs:1491–1494) — CallInfo navigation family.
- `new_userdata

`, `new_c_closure` (state.rs:1644–1645) — constructor family.

**`cargo build -p lua-cli`**: ✅ Green (war
nings only, no errors).

content.text:
Build is green.

**Report**

- **trace_call location**: `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:2083` (`pub fn trace_call`)
- **What it does**: Thin wrapper that delegates to the existing `crate::debug::trace_call`, converting the C-style `i32` (0/1) trap flag into `bool`. Called from `vm.rs:1404` at the start of each Lua frame execution to fire the call hook when debugging is active.

**Family members also implemented in the same impl block:**
- `trace_exec` (state.rs:2084) — wraps `crate::debug::trace_exec(self, pc)`, converts `i32` → `bool`. The natural sibling: same C source area (`ldebug.c`), called paired with `trace_call` in the VM dispatch loop (`vm.rs:1415`).
- `get_proto_instr` (state.rs:2080) — implemented as `ci.lua_closure().proto.code[pc]`. Not strictly the trace family, but unblocked because `crate::debug::trace_exec` calls it at `debug.rs:1816`, so trace_exec would have panicked on the first invocation. Changed generic `<T, P>` signature to concrete `(CallInfoIdx, u32)` since the lone call site (`debug.rs:1816`) already passes those types.

**Newly-surfaced todos noticed but not implemented (different families):**
- `dump_proto` (state.rs:2081) — proto serializer family, separate ticket.
- `ci_prev_instruction`, `ci_prev2_instruction`, `ci_skip_next_instruction`, `ci_step_pc_back`, `get_ci_pcrel`, `get_ci_vararg_info`, `get_ci_lua_proto_numparams` (state.rs:1538–1553) — `ci_*` accessor family.
- `CallInfo::is_vararg_func`, `set_recover_status`, `recover_status` (state.rs:312–328) — CallInfo struct family.
- `get_prev_ci`, `ci_next_func` (state.rs:1491–1494) — CallInfo navigation family.
- `new_userdata`, `new_c_closure` (state.rs:1644–1645) — constructor family.

**`cargo build -p lua-cli`**: ✅ Green (warnings only, no errors).

result:
Build is green.

**Report**

- **trace_call location**: `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:2083` (`pub fn trace_call`)
- **What it does**: Thin wrapper that delegates to the existing `crate::debug::trace_call`, converting the C-style `i32` (0/1) trap flag into `bool`. Called from `vm.rs:1404` at the start of each Lua frame execution to fire the call hook when debugging is active.

**Family members also implemented in the same impl block:**
- `trace_exec` (state.rs:2084) — wraps `crate::debug::trace_exec(self, pc)`, converts `i32` → `bool`. The natural sibling: same C source area (`ldebug.c`), called paired with `trace_call` in the VM dispatch loop (`vm.rs:1415`).
- `get_proto_instr` (state.rs:2080) — implemented as `ci.lua_closure().proto.code[pc]`. Not strictly the trace family, but unblocked because `crate::debug::trace_exec` calls it at `debug.rs:1816`, so trace_exec would have panicked on the first invocation. Changed generic `<T, P>` signature to concrete `(CallInfoIdx, u32)` since the lone call site (`debug.rs:1816`) already passes those types.

**Newly-surfaced todos noticed but not implemented (different families):**
- `dump_proto` (state.rs:2081) — proto serializer family, separate ticket.
- `ci_prev_instruction`, `ci_prev2_instruction`, `ci_skip_next_instruction`, `ci_step_pc_back`, `get_ci_pcrel`, `get_ci_vararg_info`, `get_ci_lua_proto_numparams` (state.rs:1538–1553) — `ci_*` accessor family.
- `CallInfo::is_vararg_func`, `set_recover_status`, `recover_status` (state.rs:312–328) — CallInfo struct family.
- `get_prev_ci`, `ci_next_func` (state.rs:1491–1494) — CallInfo navigation family.
- `new_userdata`, `new_c_closure` (state.rs:1644–1645) — constructor family.

**`cargo build -p lua-cli`**: ✅ Green (warnings only, no errors).
